title: Elatostema austrosinense
taxonomy: Elatostema austrosinense is classified in the family Urticaceae, commonly known as the nettle family. This family consists of flowering plants, including several species of nettle and other related genera. Elatostema austrosinense specifically belongs to the genus Elatostema, which comprises approximately 200 species of flowering plants. The genus Elatostema is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The classification of Elatostema austrosinense reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other species in the Urticaceae family.

intro: Elatostema austrosinense, also known as the Austro-Southern Polystichum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae. This plant is valued for its traditional use in Chinese medicine, where it is believed to possess health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Elatostema austrosinense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Elatostema austrosinense is commonly used in traditional Chinese herbal preparations, including decoctions and infusions, to promote health and well-being.
